title: carmichaelia crassicaulis subsp. crassicaulis
intro: Carmichaelia crassicaulis subsp. crassicaulis, also known as the thick-stemmed Carmichaelia, is a plant species in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to New Zealand, with thick, fleshy stems and pink to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The thick stems of Carmichaelia crassicaulis subsp. crassicaulis can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing and digestive support.
